body_txt: Aside from turtles , what animal would you associate with a ninja? A cat ? A wolf ? A dragon ? A... frog?! Ever since the 19th century , ninjas and frogs (or toads) have been synonymous with each other over in Japan, in part due to the influence of the epic poem-turned- Kabuki show Jiraiya Gouketsu Monogatari ("The Tale of the Gallant Jiraiya"). This ninja is almost invariably a good guy, and exhibits some kind of amazing, or even supernatural abilities, such as summoning a giant frog, turning into one themselves, or even just being a frog already . If the ninja character is a frog, you can expect a hoppy version of Walking On Water , water-based Magical Ninjutsu , and a Multipurpose Tongue put to work as a grappling tool and/or entangling weapon. Optional is to include an arch-enemy with snake powers . While it would seem rather outlandish for a stealth-based profession to give itself frog motifs, literary influence or not, frogs are stealthier and quicker than most give them credit for. Consider that the fact that frogs can catch flies — which can see changes in their environment several times faster than humans — is a testament not only to their speed, but also their patience. Also, frogs, especially aquatic ones that frequent water, are stalking predators that are able to sneak up on their prey. Their legs give them immense jumping power that allow them to pounce on their prey and back almost instantly. So, the prospect of a frog ninja must not sound so implausible to you now, huh? And aren't you glad that you pick up unusual knowledge like this from reading TV Tropes ? Compare Cat Ninja . Sometimes associated with Frog Men . Subtrope of Animal Occupation Stereotypes . For another frog occupational stereotype, see Singing Frogs .
